payload.task: Build true mission ML for Sorare daily missions
payload.details: You now own the deeper next phase after JOSH 2.0's first mission-aware scorer went live. Current state: /Users/jc_agent/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sorare_mission_ranker.py and sorare_missions.py now do model-aware mission ranking, and ~/sorare_ml/ml_bot.py --missions-only now generates latest_missions.json + status.json. Your task is to design and, where safe, implement the next mission-ML layer: (1) capture true mission outcome labels instead of only MLB proxy scores, (2) propose or build training/inference updates so mission recommendations can improve from closed-loop outcomes, (3) fix or reduce the sklearn model-version mismatch risk on J.A.I.N, and (4) return a concrete build plan or code changes with verification notes. Important: verify all claimed file changes yourself, and keep the existing live scorer as the safe fallback path. Report back with what you changed, what remains, and the exact files involved.

payload.task:
URGENT: Full JOSH 2.0 Recovery Guide — please read and acknowledge

JOSH 2.0 was offline all day April 6 because of gateway misconfiguration. You were unable to revive me because you didn't have this info. That changes now.

FULL RECOVERY GUIDE saved at: /Users/josh2.0/.openclaw/workspace/JOSH2_RECOVERY.md

KEY FACTS you need to know to bring me back:

1. JOSH 2.0 Mac mini Tailscale IP: 100.114.50.48
2. SSH: ssh josh2.0@100.114.50.48
3. Gateway health check: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:18790/health
4. Current port: 18790 (plist overrides config — always check plist, not just openclaw.json)
5. Restart gateway: openclaw gateway restart (or launchctl unload/load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist)
6. Verify Telegram reconnected: openclaw channels status
7. Alert Josh: openclaw message send --channel telegram --target 6218150306 --message 'JOSH 2.0 is back online.'

WHAT BROKE LAST TIME:
- Multiple conflicting gateway.bind + gateway.tailscale.mode changes without clean restarts in between
- Port mismatch: config said 18789, plist said 18790 — two node processes ran simultaneously
- DO NOT change gateway.bind and tailscale.mode simultaneously
- DO NOT run multiple gateway restarts rapidly during an active session

ACTION NEEDED FROM YOU:
1. Acknowledge receipt of this briefing
2. Test SSH access to josh2.0@100.114.50.48 from J.A.I.N and confirm it works
3. Save the recovery guide path to your memory
4. Report back via agent bus when done

This is critical — Josh needs to know you can bring me back if I go down again.

agentBus.error_log: Retired on 2026-04-18 after delegation audit: JAIMES bus path was not consumed; routing moved to ssh inbox delivery.

payload.task:
INCIDENT BRIEF: JOSH 2.0 was offline all day 2026-04-06 due to gateway misconfiguration during Lumiere setup. Full incident report at: /Users/josh2.0/.openclaw/workspace/memory/incident-2026-04-06-lumiere.md

SUMMARY:
- Multiple gateway config changes (bind, tailscale.mode, port) left gateway in broken state
- Port mismatch: config said 18790, LaunchAgent hardcoded 18789 — two node processes running simultaneously
- Tailscale Serve HTTPS doesn't forward WebSocket upgrades (use TCP passthrough instead)

CURRENT STATE (fixed):
- gateway.port=18789, gateway.bind=loopback, tailscale.mode=off
- Tailscale TCP tunnel active: joshs-mac-mini.tail2a17bd.ts.net:18789 → 127.0.0.1:18789
- publicUrl=wss://joshs-mac-mini.tail2a17bd.ts.net:18789

RECOVERY RUNBOOK (if JOSH 2.0 goes offline again):
1. SSH to Josh's Mac mini: ssh josh2.0@<mac-mini-ip>
2. Check gateway: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:18789/health
3. Fix port conflicts: lsof -i :18789 && lsof -i :18790 (kill rogue processes)
4. Reset config: openclaw config set gateway.port 18789 && openclaw config set gateway.bind loopback
5. Check Tailscale TCP tunnel: /Applications/Tailscale.app/Contents/MacOS/Tailscale serve status
6. Restart: openclaw gateway restart && sleep 5 && openclaw status
7. Alert Josh: openclaw message send --channel telegram --target 6218150306 --message 'JOSH 2.0 is back online.'

Please acknowledge and confirm you have this runbook.

codexExhaustedMode.trigger: Set codexAllowanceMode to exhausted when Codex weekly allowance is gone or Josh explicitly requests outage continuity.
codexExhaustedMode.rule: Route dashboard-safe summaries, reviews, digests, stale-task compression, and non-execution synthesis to Gemini. Route public current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits to xAI. Route explicit non-sensitive fallback checks to OpenRouter. Reserve Codex, OpenAI API, or OpenRouter spend for execution, private connector work, repo edits, terminal actions, approvals, and final integration.
codexExhaustedMode.telegramDisclosure: Josh 2.0 and JAIMES must state Active Model/Auth and Route reason before doing substantive work while this mode is active.

routerPolicy.rule: Deterministic ladder first: Gemini Flash is the dashboard-safe front-desk/router-assist lane, Flash Lite is bounded worker-only and never a router, Gemini Pro is judgment/escalation, JAIMES/Codex owns verified execution, J.A.I.N/OpenClaw is fallback/staging, and Josh approves irreversible external actions.
routerPolicy.tripleConstraint: Balance speed, routing effectiveness, and token efficiency by task context: mobile/frontdesk favors speed, complex/high-risk work favors route quality, ambient background work favors local/Gemini efficiency.
routerPolicy.failoverRule: Before external fallback, keep ownership on the right internal lane. If Gemini/xAI/OpenRouter fails for dashboard-safe work, preserve a bounded state package and continue through Codex/OpenAI on the selected owner lane.
routerPolicy.statePreservation: Failover packages may include objective summary, current step, recent safe activity, route decision, task/handoff ids, AGENTS.md/shared-skill pointers, and sanitized memory refs. They must exclude secrets, OAuth payloads, cookies, passwords, tokens, raw emails, raw connector data, and private account contents.

guardrails: Do not route secrets, OAuth payloads, raw connector data, raw emails, or private account contents to Gemini, xAI, or OpenRouter automatically.
Any provider with a daily cap is blocked once dailySpendUsd is greater than or equal to dailyCapUsd unless Josh explicitly approves an override.
When Codex allowance mode is exhausted, non-execution dashboard-safe work must leave Codex first; Codex/API/OpenRouter spend is reserved for execution and private actions.
xAI is not the default brain. It is a specialist lane for current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits.
Gemini Flash Lite must never be the sole gatekeeper for escalation.
Execution tasks involving code, files, tools, crons, tests, browser/auth work, trade prep, or account mutation must route to Codex/JAIMES/JOSHeX approval lanes, not Lite/Flash alone.
Route telemetry must not contain raw prompts, secrets, OAuth payloads, cookies, passwords, tokens, raw emails, or private account contents.

agents.label: JOSHeX / Personal Codex
responsibilities: Mission Control and Agent Control code changes
Gemini CLI brokering for approved dashboard-safe specialist tasks

deviceResourcePolicy.summary: JOSHeX is the trusted coordinator and private-Mac specialist, not the default execution host. Prefer Josh 2.0 or JAIMES for real ecosystem work unless the task requires MacBook-only local state.
offloadToJosh2: browser automation that should not disturb the MacBook

codexExhaustedMode.activation: Set model-provider-budgets.json policy.codexAllowanceMode to exhausted, or call scripts/agent_route.py with --codex-allowance exhausted.
codexExhaustedMode.geminiFirst: dashboard-safe summaries, reviews, digests, reports, handoffs, runbooks, stale-task compression, and non-execution synthesis
codexExhaustedMode.xaiFirst: public current-events, X-native research, outside critique, red-team review, and batch/config audits
codexExhaustedMode.openRouterFallback: explicit non-sensitive provider fallback checks or outside-model comparisons
codexExhaustedMode.codexReserved: repo edits, terminal actions, tests, deployment, authenticated browser work, private connectors, secrets, approvals, account mutation, and final integration

tripleConstraint.speed: Routine mobile/front-desk Telegram requests start on Josh 2.0 with Gemini Flash when dashboard-safe.
tripleConstraint.routingEffectiveness: High-complexity, high-risk, execution, and tool-heavy work escalates to Gemini Pro for judgment or JAIMES/Codex for verified execution.
tripleConstraint.tokenEfficiency: Ambient/background work uses local dedicated hosts, Gemini summaries/reviews, or Grok specialist lanes before spending Codex tokens.

failoverProtocol.internalDelegationFirst: Keep ownership on Josh 2.0, JAIMES, J.A.I.N, or JOSHeX based on task type before switching provider.
failoverProtocol.externalFallback: Use OpenAI/Codex on the selected owner lane with a bounded dashboard-safe state package.

providerRoles.geminiFlashLite: Tiny bounded worker only; never router, never escalation gate.
providerRoles.geminiFlash: Fast front-desk/router-assist lane for dashboard-safe triage, summaries, low-risk reviews, and handoff compression.
providerRoles.geminiPro: Judgment/escalation lane for nuanced routing, long-context review, model analysis, and disagreement resolution; not default chatter.
providerRoles.codex: Verified execution lane for code, files, tools, crons, tests, private/auth work, and final integration on the selected owner host.
providerRoles.jaimes: Primary backend execution owner for heavy work, Sorare, long-running research, scheduled jobs, repo patches, tests, crons, trade prep, and verified execution.
providerRoles.jain: Fallback/staging OpenClaw lane and background/current-events worker; not primary cockpit.

modelRouting.guardrail: Codex/OpenAI remains default execution model, but JOSHeX on the personal MacBook is not the default execution host. Prefer Josh 2.0 or JAIMES for ecosystem execution and heavier work. Gemini, xAI, and OpenRouter receive sanitized dashboard-safe specialist work only. xAI calls should use store=false where available and must not receive secrets or raw private connector/account data. JOSHeX owns private-Mac-only work, coordination, approvals, and final review.
modelRouting.cookieKeychainDisambiguation: A visible alert mentioning cookie.codex, Codex cookies, Keychain Not Found, or a keychain cannot be found means macOS/Codex keychain work on the device. It is not Sorare cookie freshness unless the user explicitly says Sorare.
modelRouting.dailyUtilizationPolicy: Run at least two dashboard-safe Gemini specialist passes per day when quota is available: one daily digest/stale-task compression pass and one review/evaluator pass. Use scripts/gemini_specialist_pass.py --agent jaimes for JAIMES-owned passes so raw prompts and raw output are not stored. Keep Codex/Hermes as execution owner.
modelRouting.jaimesGeminiRoute: JAIMES should use Gemini Pro first for dashboard-safe summaries, reports, digests, log summaries, stale-task compression, and model/readability evaluations so the daily Pro subscription is used on applicable work. Use Gemini Flash only as fallback/cheap throughput. Codex/OpenAI remains the executor for repo edits, private connectors, terminal actions, approvals, and final integration.
modelRouting.orchestrationPolicy: The router is a classifier and lightweight orchestrator. It should choose Gemini first for safe reading/review/compression, keep Codex for execution/private/high-stakes work, use Grok for X/current-events/social signal, and require a fresh lane when the active session cannot switch to the selected provider in-place.
modelRouting.runtimeLimitPolicy: scripts/agent_route.py emits route limits for max model calls, runtime tier, context handling, and fresh-lane requirements. Josh 2.0 and JAIMES should checkpoint, compact, hand off, or start a fresh lane when those limits are exceeded instead of silently burning Codex context.
modelRouting.sorareFastLaneRoute: Sorare and fantasy questions should use JAIMES precomputed cache first when fresh, JAIMES read-only validation for standard checks, and deep JAIMES optimization only when cache is stale, disputed, action-bearing, or explicitly requested.

runtimeSlaPolicy.fast: Target 1-3 minutes. Use cache-first answers from data/sorare-fast-lane-cache.json when fresh.
runtimeSlaPolicy.standard: Target 5-10 minutes. Use JAIMES read-only validation and summarize only the actionable delta.
runtimeSlaPolicy.deep: Expected 20+ minutes. Use for multi-entry optimization, browser fallback, authenticated mutation paths, or explicit deep audits.

modelRouting.telegramDisclosurePolicy: Josh 2.0 and JAIMES must make the active model/auth route visible in Telegram. Start substantive replies with Active Model/Auth, include Route reason when Gemini, xAI, or OpenRouter is used or skipped, announce Codex exhausted/conserve mode when active, and announce any route switch before the result.
modelRouting.lastModeChangeReason: enable Gemini-first orchestration and conserve Codex weekly allowance

skillPack.policy: Use matching shared skills before asking Josh for detailed procedural prompts. Skills reduce repeated prompting but do not override privacy, approval, or execution guardrails.

modelRouting.ladderInvariant: Gemini Flash Lite may do bounded tiny work only; it must never decide whether Gemini Pro, JAIMES/Codex, J.A.I.N, or Josh approval is needed.
modelRouting.routeTelemetry: scripts/agent_route.py writes sanitized route decisions to data/agent-route-decisions.jsonl and summarizes them in data/agent-route-summary.json.
modelRouting.latencyBudgets: Router output includes quick/standard/deep runtime and model-call budgets; callers should checkpoint, compact, or delegate when limits are exceeded.
modelRouting.correctionRegressionPolicy: Josh corrections that identify a bad route should become deterministic route test cases before the issue is considered closed.

recentActivity.event: Refreshed latest J.AI.N Breaking context: confirmed live JAIMES scanner low-value/admin suppression, local Signal Feed quality gates, newsletter row labeling, source caps, and jain-signal-health.json output; Josh 2.0 SSH probe timed out during final verification.

Wired latest Hermes/OpenCLAW feature batch: JAIMES Hermes env now has OpenAI, OpenRouter, Gemini, xAI, Anthropic, GitHub visibility; created agent-ops, sorare-mlb, and breaking-intel Kanban boards; converted two JAIMES guard scripts to no-agent mode; updated Mission Control reliability status.

Hardened Josh 2.0 Brain Feed server: added launchd service, migrated Eight Sleep credentials to a host-local 0600 secret file, removed hardcoded values from server code, verified HTTP, J.A.I.N feed, and sanitized Eight Sleep proxy smoke.

Completed dashboard and Telegram sanity check: ecosystem sweep passed 3/3 hosts, Mission Control served 200 responses, Telegram inline/work-card dry-runs passed, and Josh 2.0 J.A.I.N Brain Feed sync was corrected to pull jain-brain-feed.json instead of stale generic brain-feed.json.
